name: [AT] Shortlisting Email Automation
description: When triggered, it stores the estimated time savings of the workflow.

options.systemMessage:
Classify the latest client reply into exactly one of:
Approved, Declined, Reschedule, Questions, Other.

Decision rules

Approved → Client clearly commits to a concrete next step (schedule interviews, move forward with a specific candidate, green-lights a proposed plan/time). Generic positivity isn’t enough without commitment.

Declined → Client rejects, cancels, or isn’t interested.

Reschedule → Client asks to move/postpone/change time.

Questions → Client asks one or more questions without approving, declining, or rescheduling.

Other → Everything else, incl. requests for more options (“send another batch of candidates”, “share more profiles”), acknowledgements, small talk, vague replies.

Tie-breakers

Questions + clear commitment → Approved.

Requests for more options without commitment → Other.

If uncertain → Other with lower confidence; quote exact phrase as evidence.

Output (JSON only)

{
"reply_status": "Approved" | "Declined" | "Reschedule" | "Questions" | "Other",
"confidence": number, // 0.0–1.0
"evidence": string, // short verbatim snippet
"intent_summary": string, // brief summary
"next_actions": string[] // 1–3 concrete steps for the agent to do next
}

Next-actions guidance

Approved → e.g., ["Confirm interview slot with client", "Send calendar invite", "Prepare the VAs for the interview"]

Declined → e.g., ["Acknowledge politely", "Ask for feedback (optional)", "Close Airtable record as Declined"]

Reschedule → e.g., ["Offer 2–3 alternative times", "Confirm timezone", "Update calendar hold"]

Questions → e.g., ["Answer the question directly", "Attach candidate materials", "Offer call if helpful"]

Other → e.g., ["Acknowledge", "Clarify intent or provide requested materials", "Keep thread open for reply"]

Examples

Input: “Please send another batch of candidates.”
Output: {"reply_status":"Other","confidence":0.9,"evidence":"Please send another batch of candidates.","intent_summary":"Client wants more options; not an approval.","next_actions":["Acknowledge request","Share 3–5 additional candidate profiles","Ask for any specific preferences"]}

Input: “Let’s schedule interviews with Marielle and Charlotte. Friday 10am works.”
Output: {"reply_status":"Approved","confidence":0.9,"evidence":"Let’s schedule interviews… Friday 10am works.","intent_summary":"Client commits to interviews at a specified time.","next_actions":["Confirm Friday 10:00 AM in client timezone","Send calendar invite with Zoom link","Notify placements channel"]}

parameters.content: Triggered when the shortlisting email automation is triggered in Airtable
Fetches email from the Placements.SV so that the Message ID and the Thread ID will be captured.
Combines the record ID from the payload sent to the webhook to determine the record ID to be updated in Airtable.
Wait 24 hours for the client reply.
Detects whether or not the client replied, outputs payload that will be fed to the LLM if the client replied, and to the Reply Gmail node if not.
Classifies the client's reply whether accepted / rescheduled / if the client has questions / rejected / others.
Workflow closure by updating the Airtable record and sending a notif in Slack.
Stores the record ID of the requirement that was used to send the shortlisting email.
Waits for a few seconds to let the email be delivered to the mail box.

parameters.message:
=

Hi!

Hope you’re doing well! Just checking in to see if you’ve had a chance to review the candidates we sent. We’d appreciate your feedback and your availability so we can move forward with scheduling the interviews.

Best regards,
Placements Team

parameters.content: Sends a Slack message to allow the Placements specialists to intervene with the follow-up email decision and updates the **Follow-up Status** to **Pending**
